summary: The Goshen Township Zoning Commission held a public hearing on case ZC 2023-001, a proposal by Domino's (represented by Dryden Builders) to rezone a 2.17-acre vacant parcel at 6200 State Route 28 from residential (R3) to commercial use for a 2,036 square foot drive-thru restaurant. Residents raised significant concerns about traffic on Patricia Boulevard and State Route 28, as well as stormwater drainage issues. The commission voted to continue the case pending completion of a traffic impact study and better clarification on drainage plans. The commission also elected officers, approved previous meeting minutes, adopted 2023 meeting dates, and welcomed new member Brandon.
decisions: Approved video/meeting minutes from November 1st hearing
Elected vice chair (appears to be Lewis)
Motion to continue case ZC 2023-001 pending traffic study and drainage clarification - passed unanimously
action_items: Applicant (Dryden Builders/Choice One Engineering) to complete traffic impact study and submit to county engineer's office
Applicant to contact ODOT regarding potential impacts or access to State Route 28
Applicant (Caliber Engineering) to provide better clarification on stormwater drainage plan
Gary Holbrook to send new notification to parties regarding rescheduled Board of Trustees meeting
Applicant to address all 22 staff recommendations on next plan submission
Zoning commission seeking applications for one zoning commission member and one alternate
key_amounts: 2.17 acres total parcel size proposed to be split into 1.17 acres and 1.00 acre
Building footprint 34 by 56 feet with 5-foot pop-out (39 x 56 overall)
Over 1,900 acres total in corridor study area
Approximately 4 feet of fall across the site
notable_topics: Rezoning of vacant land from R3 residential to B2 general business for Domino's drive-thru restaurant
State Route 28 corridor plan goals and objectives
Traffic concerns on Patricia Boulevard and State Route 28 - difficulty turning left, no traffic signal
Stormwater detention/drainage concerns and impact on residential properties
Lot split proposal into two separate parcels
Distinction between detention vs retention ponds and underground detention alternatives
ODOT previously denied deceleration lane and road widening requests
Election of zoning commission officers for 2023
Child safety concerns regarding detention pond near daycare/kinder care
attendees: Chris Tinkle (Dryden Builders Inc., general contractor/applicant representative)
Wayne Whittaker / Anthony Whittaker (resident, 6200 block Patricia Blvd)
notes: The Clermont County Regional Planning Commission unanimously approved the project on January 23, 2023 with staff recommendations. A public records request under Ohio Revised Code 149.43 was filed and partially fulfilled, with some documents withheld under attorney-client privilege. The fire chief indicated no issues with site access but may require a fire hydrant depending on suppression system. The applicant emphasized the drive-thru is more of a 'pickup window' for online orders with minimal stacking (2-3 cars max). A prior applicant attempted to develop this property 10-15 years ago and a traffic study at that time recommended a turning lane. The next meeting was scheduled for March 7, 2023 at 6:30 PM, and the related Board of Trustees meeting would be rescheduled accordingly.
